notes.anchor: with three armies as strong as these
notes.note: The three armies are those of Decimus Brutus, of the consuls' forces now under Octavian, and of Plancus (with Lepidus's still in the balance); Decimus urges Cicero to take heart in the combined Republican strength after the relief of Mutina.

notes.anchor: If you take the bit between your teeth
notes.note: A metaphor from horsemanship (Latin frenum momorderis, "champ the bit") meaning to throw off restraint and act boldly; Decimus eggs Cicero on to speak out in the Senate without fear.

Eporedia (modern Ivrea) was a Roman colony in Cisalpine Gaul at the foot of the Alpine passes; Decimus Brutus wrote from there while pursuing Antony northward. The Latin dateline "viii K. Iunias" (the eighth day before the Kalends of June, counted inclusively) is 25 May 43 BC.
